title: Capitation reweighting
evidence_role: Confirms capitation is core way general practice is funded; fixed annual amount per enrolled person; reweighting from 1 July 2026; factors include age, sex, multimorbidity, rurality and deprivation.
incorporation: Used to clarify that capitation is a fixed-population payment and does not create a demand-led marginal contact stream.

title: Cabinet material: Primary Health Care Funding Improvements and Update on Primary Health Care Strategic Priorities
evidence_role: Shows current reform includes funding improvements and strategic priorities; not simply legacy capitation.
incorporation: Used to keep current reform as comparator.

title: Cabinet paper: Primary health care funding improvements
evidence_role: States reweighting is a key step; mentions strengthening accountability and aligning funding and incentives; capitation remains main funding source.
incorporation: Used to contrast current reform with proposed open-ended FFS entitlement stream.

title: National Primary Care Dataset
publisher: Health New Zealand | Te Whatu Ora
evidence_role: Dataset will collect appointment/encounter information to inform 80 percent within seven days target.
incorporation: Used to specify data needed to monitor uncapped FFS activity, access and gaming.

title: ACC: Paying you for your services
evidence_role: ACC payments depend on contract, legislation/regulation or agreement; Cost of Treatment Regulations specify who is paid, how much ACC contributes and treatment types; contracts and purchase orders also exist.
incorporation: Used as the ACC-style analogue for demand-led, rules-based treatment contributions.

title: ACC provider obligations under Cost of Treatment Regulations
evidence_role: Services must be clinically necessary, appropriate, quality/performance compliant, appropriate duration/frequency, within scope, and billed only for covered injuries.
incorporation: Used to define controls that replace global caps: eligibility, scope, clinical necessity, frequency/duration and audit.
